response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with 20+ years across web, mobile, and some systems work, but he misses nearly every hard requirement for this role: there is no Japanese proficiency listed (native-level is mandatory), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, no embedded LAN-switch or network-security background (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), and his C experience is brief and dates to 2007-2008. His recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js product and AI-tooling work in Berlin, which does not transfer to embedded firmware security development.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with genuine systems exposure (DLang low-latency services, C/C++ at National Instruments)
Strong software-craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship
Comfortable on Linux and with performance-sensitive, asynchronous I/O code
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed, failing a non-negotiable native-level requirement
No embedded networking or security-feature experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switch development)
C-for-UNIX and kernel-level experience is minimal and roughly 18 years old; recent work is web/full-stack

key_factors.factor: No embedded network-security or Linux kernel experience
Broad senior engineering experience with some C/C++ and Linux systems background
